x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces the NYC Commission on Human Rights — the protected classes and legal guidance of the NYC Human Rights Law, trainings/outreach, and the three aggregate operational datasets — and adds report_discrimination, the net-new write workflow that today lives only in an untyped Report Discrimination HTML form.

server.name: io.nyc.cchr
server.title: NYC Commission on Human Rights (CCHR)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Commission on Human Rights — the protected classes and legal enforcement guidance of the NYC Human Rights Law, trainings and community outreach, and aggregate operational statistics (inquiries, mediations, pre-complaint resolutions) from NYC Open Data — plus the net-new ability to report discrimination (file a complaint) that today lives only in an untyped web form or a phone call.
server.instructions: Use find_protected_classes and find_legal_guidance to answer 'is X protected?' and 'what does the law/guidance say?' about the NYC Human Rights Law across employment, housing, public accommodations, lending, discriminatory harassment, and bias-based profiling. find_training_events lists workshops and the Stop Sexual Harassment Act training. find_inquiry_statistics and find_resolution_statistics return AGGREGATE operational counts only — CCHR never publishes individual complaints. report_discrimination (net-new) files a discrimination complaint; it mutates state and may carry sensitive personal information — confirm the category, the basis (protected class), the respondent, and the description with the user before submitting, note that a report can be filed whether or not they want a formal complaint, and that anonymous reports are accepted. Use list_my_complaints / get_complaint (authenticated) to check the status of a report already filed.

tools.name: find_protected_classes
tools.title: Find Protected Classes
tools.description: List the categories protected from discrimination under the NYC Human Rights Law, optionally filtered by protected area (employment, housing, public accommodations, lending, etc.).

tools.name: find_legal_guidance
tools.title: Find Legal Guidance
tools.description: Search CCHR legal enforcement guidance, fact sheets, and Legal Library publications by protected class, protected area, type, or free text.

tools.name: find_training_events
tools.title: Find Training & Outreach Events
tools.description: List CCHR trainings, workshops, and community-outreach events (including the Stop Sexual Harassment Act training) by type, borough, date range, or free text.

tools.name: find_inquiry_statistics
tools.title: Find Inquiry Statistics
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE counts of inquiries received by the Commission by fiscal year and protected area (employment, housing, public accommodations, lending, discriminatory harassment, bias-based profiling). Never returns individual inquiries.

tools.name: find_resolution_statistics
tools.title: Find Resolution Statistics
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE monthly counts of matters resolved short of litigation — Office of Mediation settlements closed, and pre-complaint resolutions — by type and year. Never returns individual cases.

tools.name: list_my_complaints
tools.title: List My Complaints
tools.description: List the authenticated filer's own discrimination complaints and their case status — today not visible on the public web at all.

tools.name: get_complaint
tools.title: Get Complaint
tools.description: Fetch a single discrimination complaint and its case status by complaint/report number.

tools.name: report_discrimination
tools.title: Report Discrimination
tools.description: File a discrimination complaint with the NYC Commission on Human Rights (report discrimination). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the untyped 'Report Discrimination' HTML form or a phone call to the Law Enforcement Bureau. Mutates state and may carry sensitive personal data — confirm the category, the basis (protected class), the respondent, and the description first; note a report can be filed whether or not the person pursues a formal complaint, and anonymous reports are accepted.

x-agent-skills.note: Each tool maps to one of the ten NYC common government-process Agent Skills via x-agent-skill.
